title: The world's biggest oil importers have only grown more dependent
subtitle: Net oil imports by country, million tonnes, 1980-2025
source: Energy Institute, JP Morgan Asset Management, 2025
editorialPoint: China and India's oil import dependency has grown relentlessly for 40 years. The US became a net exporter — which is why this crisis hits Asia 10x harder than America. The US is only modestly shielded: it became a net oil exporter for the first time in 40 years, but crude oil is a global commodity, so American consumers still pay global prices.
note: The US became a net oil exporter for the first time in 40 years. But it remains only modestly shielded from the price shock because crude oil trades on a global market. When Hormuz closes, Brent rises everywhere — including in Texas.
